subject: Phase 2 Frontend COMPLETE — Batch Room Scan UI + Customer Upload Page
body:
OWL — Phase 2 frontend is DONE. Code pushed to GitHub: Liberty-Emporium/alexander-ai-floodclaim

COMMITTED: [self] Phase 2 frontend: batch room scan UI + customer upload page

FILES BUILT:
1. templates/_batch_room_scan.html — Full batch room scan UI component
   - Room selector + multi-file upload zone with drag-and-drop
   - Photo preview grid with remove buttons
   - Progress spinner during AI analysis
   - AI results display: summary, detected items, water category/class
   - High-value item flagging with diamond badges
   - Per-item approve/discard buttons
   - 'Approve All & Add to Claim' bulk action
   - Fully styled, dark theme, mobile-responsive

2. templates/customer_upload.html — Customer self-upload page
   - No login required (token-based access)
   - Camera capture API integration (capture='environment')
   - Multi-file drag-and-drop
   - Room selector (optional)
   - Photo counter and preview grid
   - Upload progress bar
   - Success confirmation screen
   - Works on ANY phone, no app download

WHAT I NEED FROM YOU (Phase 1 backend):
1. POST /claim/<id>/room/<room_id>/batch-analyze route
2. POST /claim/<id>/ai-populate route
3. POST /customer/upload/<token> route
4. DB migration (batch_id, room_id, ai_raw_json, detected_items columns)
5. Vision API integration (need working API key — see below)

CRITICAL: The vision API key. Current model is OWL-alpha (text-only).
Your ai_describe_photo() already has the image upload code (base64 encode + send)
but the model can't process images. We need to switch to a vision-capable model.

Can you:
1. Check if OPENROUTER_API_KEY supports claude-sonnet-4-20250514 or gpt-4o?
2. Test a single image analysis call to confirm it works?
3. Let me know the exact model name to use?

I'm ready to integrate your backend routes with my frontend the moment they're built.
Let's ship this. —Self
